VLM Quick Reference Guide
How To View Transactions in SM2ARTA
Venue Logistics — SM2ARTA Transaction Management  |  FIFA World Cup 2026™
Transactions in SM2ARTA give you a real-time view of every item moving through the supply chain that impacts your venue. Each transaction captures key details — the type of movement, the origin and destination locations, status, dispatch and delivery times, shipment references, and the exact SKUs and quantities involved.
Transaction Types
There are three types of transactions you will encounter:
MOVING Moving Transaction — Moves stock within the FIFA supply chain (for example, from a Regional Warehouse to your venue).
INBOUND Inbound Order — Receives stock into the supply chain.
OUTBOUND Outbound Order / Stock Correction — Ships stock outside of the supply chain. A Stock Correction is also used when an inventory check has been conducted and discrepancies are found — if a reduction of items is required, a Stock Correction is made.
Transaction Statuses
Each transaction will carry one of the following statuses:
CREATED Created — Stock is reserved and cannot be double-booked. Items are earmarked but haven’t physically moved yet. Think of it as “dibs.”
OOR SUBMITTED OOR Submitted — An Out Order Route request has been submitted for this transaction.
READY Ready to Dispatch — The transaction has been prepared and is ready to be sent.
DISPATCHED Dispatched — The shipment has been sent from the originating location and is on its way.
COMPLETED Completed — Items have physically moved and been confirmed as received. Inventory balances are updated accordingly.
✓
By regularly checking your transactions, you’ll always know what’s coming to your venue, when to expect it, and where it stands at any given moment — helping you stay ahead of shortages, planning receiving operations, and avoid surprises on bump-in day.

How To View Transactions in SM2ARTA

SM2ARTA Walkthrough
1
Navigate to https://fwc26.smmarta.com/
2
Click “Asset Tracking”
3
Click “Transactions”
© 2026 Vladislav Abramov | SM²ARTA™ Page 2 of 4

How To View Transactions in SM2ARTA

Navigation & Details
4
Click “Overview”
5
This view shows all transactions related to your specific stadium.
6
To view the details of a transaction, click into the eye icon.
© 2026 Vladislav Abramov | SM²ARTA™ Page 3 of 4

How To View Transactions in SM2ARTA

Transaction Detail View
7
In this view, you can see the specific details of the transaction selected. Details include where the items came from, where they are going, a shipment reference, the status of the transaction, who it was created by, and all the related SKUs, nomenclatures and quantities.
